prompt: I have a compartmental model with 4 parameters: infection rate beta (positive), recovery rate gamma (positive), initial infected fraction i0 (between 0 and 1), and reporting probability p (between 0 and 1). The simulator generates daily case counts for 30 days. I want to train an amortized estimator with BayesFlow that correctly handles the parameter constraints. Set up the full workflow.
assertions: Defines prior with correct support for all 4 parameters (beta>0, gamma>0, 0<i0<1, 0<p<1)
Uses bf.Adapter() with explicit constraint handling
Calls .constrain() with lower=0 for beta and gamma
Calls .constrain() with lower=0, upper=1 for i0 and p
Routes the daily case counts through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
Uses a summary network appropriate for sequential data (TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or SetTransformer if treating days as exchangeable is justified and documented)
Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
Sets summary_dim to approximately 2x the number of parameters (around 8)
Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Runs in-silico diagnostics before any real-data inference
Checks all 4 parameters individually against house thresholds
Does NOT flatten the time series into inference_conditions
